main_code_change: Original adversarial diffusion completion; stronger adv/consistency weights; no full multi-missing evaluation file
missing_eye_0.3_denoise_acc: Best is far better than final, showing later overfitting/instability; lacks full sample/denoise evaluation table
missing_eye_0.3_denoise_macro_f1: Use only as early baseline if needed; not enough for main ablation table

main_code_change: Lower adversarial/consistency weights, warmup, spectral norm, critical U-Net attention, cosine LR, early stopping, eval interval; old MLP fusion
main_observation: Best denoise result is strongest among non-slot baselines, but sample mode drops sharply, especially missing_eeg
decision_for_paper: Use as important baseline; supports claim that denoise diagnostic is not enough

main_code_change: Added sampling-time conditional guidance with guidance_scale 1.5
main_observation: Both denoise and sample are worse than improved baseline; guidance was not trained with proper conditional dropout
decision_for_paper: Negative ablation; explain that naive guidance is ineffective

main_code_change: Added DDIM as optional reverse sampler; trained/evaluated DDIM run
current_code_status: default restored to SDE; DDIM kept optional only
main_observation: DDIM does not improve sample quality and slightly weakens denoise average; SDE remains safer
decision_for_paper: Report as sampler ablation; keep SDE as default

main_code_change: Replaced final concat MLP fusion with fixed EEG/Eye/Availability Slot Fusion Transformer; SDE default
main_observation: Sample average improves over MLP baseline, especially missing_eeg/missing_eye sample, but denoise classification decreases
decision_for_paper: Use as current main structural variant if paper emphasizes target-free sample completion

main_code_change: Added extra proxy views, proxy alignment/classification, prototype distribution consistency
main_observation: Proxy supervision increases constraint complexity but hurts both sample and denoise compared with Slot Fusion + SDE
decision_for_paper: Negative ablation; do not keep in final code

main_code_change: Injected fixed band adjacency and approximate channel topology prior into EEG encoder
main_observation: Random 0.3 denoise and EEG reconstruction improve, but full average and missing_eeg classification do not improve stably
decision_for_paper: Use as negative/partial ablation: improves EEG reconstruction, not final classification

main_code_change: Added graph-prior re-encoding consistency for generated EEG latents on top of graph prior
main_observation: Missing_eeg denoise improves but sample mode becomes much worse; stronger graph constraint overfits diagnostic path
decision_for_paper: Negative ablation; useful to argue reconstruction/denoise gains do not guarantee sample completion gains

main_code_change: Compared Slot Fusion + SDE under missing_rate 0.3 and 0.5 for random/missing_eeg/missing_eye and sample/denoise
main_observation: Only random mode is mathematically affected by missing_rate in current mask implementation; fixed missing_eeg/missing_eye ignore missing_rate and therefore change little or not at all
decision_for_paper: Use this file to explain missing-rate robustness and why fixed missing-mode results are nearly unchanged

main_code_change: At evaluation/sample mode, draw 3 stochastic SDE completions, estimate latent variance, convert variance to confidence, and down-weight high-uncertainty generated latents in fusion
current_code_status: kept optional; disabled by default with uncertainty_samples=1
training_setting: Evaluation-only on runs/eeg_eye_adv_slot_sde/best_model.pth; uncertainty_samples=3, uncertainty_temperature=0.1, sample mode only
main_observation: Compared with Slot Fusion + SDE sample avg, acc drops 0.6936->0.6802 and macro_f1 drops 0.6794->0.6678. MSE decreases on average, but cosine similarity and classification degrade; confidence collapses to min 0.05, so generated latents are over-downweighted.
decision_for_paper: Negative ablation in current form. It supports the point that uncertainty gating alone cannot solve sample generation; it needs calibration or training-time uncertainty supervision before being used as a contribution.

main_code_change: Stage3 latter-half sample classification loss, sample-to-teacher KL distillation, and optional SDE/DDIM sampling self-conditioning
current_code_status: kept in code as optional/default training path
training_setting: 10/10/40 stages, random missing 0.3, eval_mode sample, lambda_sample_cls=0.2, lambda_sample_distill=0.05, self_conditioning_sample=true
main_observation: Compared with Slot Fusion + SDE, sample macro_f1 slightly improves on average 0.6794->0.6811 and random 0.3 sample improves 0.7273->0.7446, but missing_eeg sample drops 0.6398->0.6232. Denoise average also decreases. The optimization helps random/missing_eye sample adaptation but hurts Eye-to-EEG completion.
decision_for_paper: Partial ablation. Useful evidence that sample-path training helps target-free random/missing_eye sampling, but it needs missing_eeg-aware weighting or direction-specific training before becoming the final main model.
